item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this SCP because I'm a huge fan of body horror in general, but I'm having trouble picturing what the very first modification would look like. Would the limbs be protruding from the front of the torso? I assume the arms positions were changed due to their amputation afterwards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't fully follows up on the implications it raises in how the SCP manifests/demanifests and the logs were gratuitous. If there was a rhyme or reason as to what they were trying to achieve, even if it was a failed attempt at medical treatment, I didn't get it. There's more story to be mined in giving it an orange/grey interpretation of what counts as a medical emergency. You could also maybe throw in some classist elements in terms of whether or not people can afford ambulances having anything to do with how these entities manifest. But it's just straightforward and violent and there doesn't seem to be any reason for it. The body modifications are creative but they're not enough to make me enjoy this.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: So sorry, that was completely my bad! I've replaced the image in question.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like a sweet magnolia tree, this SCP blossomed tenderly.

This was an excellent example of showing, not telling. A +1,

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Are we allowed to use BY-NC-SA photos?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You go away for like, a year and return with a crop of new scips.

You're like a farmer.

+1.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to TheMightyMcB, Oboebandgeek99 and Mew-ltiverse for their feedback in chat!

PLEASE DON’T SUE TANHONY

Ambulance picture: https://search.creativecommons.org/photos/73aa1941-15e7-4eef-bd46-98a2f3254e50 (CC BY-SA 2.0)
